title: Receiving Cash Before the Work
event: A client pays Tran Consulting a $3,000 retainer for services to be performed next quarter. Prepare the entry.

entries.label: Cash now, obligation now, revenue later

lines.label: received in advance
lines.why: The money is real and it's yours to hold.
lines.trap: Holding cash and having earned it are different things — the credit line is where that shows.

lines.label: work still owed
lines.why: You OWE the work — that's a liability until it's performed.
lines.trap: Not revenue yet. Crediting Service Revenue here overstates income and hides the obligation.

memorize.body: Unearned Revenue is a LIABILITY with 'revenue' in its name — the most treacherous account title in the course.
It converts to real revenue later via the adjusting entry, as the work actually gets done.

questions.prompt: Revenue recognized when the $3,000 retainer arrives = ?

distractors.feedback: Cash before work = a liability (Unearned Revenue). It converts to revenue only as the work is performed.

build.scaffold: Cash before work → Unearned Revenue (liability). Work before cash → Accounts Receivable (asset).
